policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 959 tests and measured the identical 1762-line core scope at 1438 covered lines after the independently reviewed issue #130 correction. The reviewed high-water is unchanged: routing tag-renderer storage through the fault-containing browser-storage adapter added three executable core lines, two covered and one recovery branch outside the existing tag-renderer tests; the follow-up registered-activation, canonical-integer, and pre-auth-diagnostic fixes live in the classic loader and test harness and therefore do not alter this core scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only negligible instrumentation variance.

Three clean reviewed local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 1559 tests and measured the identical 26110-line assembly scope at 17508, 17509, and 17509 covered lines; the reviewed high-water is 17509. The completed #153 scope replaces Calendar's response-dependent fail-open root inference and first-provider-edition selection with one user-scoped tri-state resolver. It preserves every provider candidate, selects only accessible type-correct editions, obtains authoritative Arr root folders per exact instance and media type, applies most-specific Jellyfin folder policy, and treats ambiguous, malformed, cross-instance, cross-type, or otherwise unresolved paths as inaccessible while filtering is enabled. Shared authorization-query construction configures Jellyfin's user library scope before assigning a distinct batched ItemIds set, and provider, access, root-folder, and user-data work remains batched rather than per-event. Deterministic regressions cover inaccessible and unknown upcoming-only responses; accessible, inaccessible, and unresolved states; duplicate inaccessible-first/accessible-second editions and episode user data; input reordering; Unix and Windows roots, filesystem roots, dot segments, path boundaries, overlapping folders, root/provider collisions across Arr instances and media types; inaccessible-instance metadata suppression; safe authorization-query ordering in both consumers; and deliberate unfiltered parity when the access filter is disabled. The existing two-line tolerance is unchanged and covers only the reproduced one-line Coverlet instrumentation variance between otherwise identical clean runs (about 0.0038 percentage points).
